title: NOC 73311 – Railway conductors and brakemen/women | GTR Immigration
description: NOC 73311 professionals are the operational coordinators of Canada’s rail traffic. As a TEER 3 occupation, Railway Conductors and Brakemen/women are responsible for the safe movement of freight and passenger trains, managing crew activities, and executing complex switching operations. In 2026, as Canada scales its Supply Chain Resilience Strategy and expands intermodal freight hubs, these professionals are classified as Essential Logistics Talent. For immigration, this role is a prime entry point into the high-paying rail sector and is highly targeted in the 2026 Transport Category-Based Selection for Express Entry.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category7 – Trades, transport and equipment operators and related occupationsTEER Level3Major Group73 – General tradesSub-Major Group733 – Transportation occupationsMinor Group7331 – Train crew operating occupationsNOC Code73311

duties: Crew Coordination: Supervise and coordinate the activities of passenger and freight train crews to ensure on-time performance and safety.
Order Execution: Receive and interpret train orders, radio messages, and signals from dispatchers to coordinate train movements.
Safety Inspection: Conduct mandatory inspections of train cars to ensure all mechanical components, air brakes, and couplings are secure before departure.
Switching Operations: Operate manual or remote-control switches to move cars between different tracks for loading, unloading, or train assembly.
Brake Management: Set and release handbrakes and connect air brake hoses between cars to ensure full control of the train's braking system.
Cargo Security: Verify that all freight is properly secured and that dangerous goods are handled according to strict TDG (Transportation of Dangerous Goods) regulations.
Passenger Service: (Passenger Trains) Coordinate with onboard staff to ensure passenger safety, manage boarding, and handle emergency situations.
Yard Management: (Yard Conductors) Oversee the makeup and breakup of trains in industrial yards using switching lists and yard manifests.
Emergency Response: Lead the initial response to mechanical failures or track obstructions, coordinating with maintenance crews and dispatchers.
Documentation: Maintain accurate digital logs of car movements, trip delays, and mechanical issues encountered during the shift.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is the standard requirement.
Training: Completion of a specialized training program (often 3 to 6 months) provided by the railway company is mandatory.
Certification: A valid Canadian Rail Operating Rules (CROR) certificate is required (usually obtained through employer training).
Physical Fitness: Candidates must pass rigorous medical, vision, and hearing exams, along with mandatory drug and alcohol screening.
Schedule Flexibility: Conductors must be willing to work "On-Call" schedules, including nights, weekends, and holidays, often spending time away from home.
TDG Certification: Required for those handling hazardous materials or dangerous goods.
salary_prospects: Note: In 2026, experienced Railway Conductors on long-haul freight routes often earn annual salaries between $85,000 and $115,000 inclusive of mileage premiums and overtime.
job_outlook: The outlook is Favourable. Projections indicate that the shift toward rail for long-haul freight (due to its lower carbon footprint compared to trucking) will sustain high demand. While some yard automation is increasing, the complex safety and logistical decisions required for main-track operations ensure that conductors remain a critical, human-centered requirement for the next decade.
labour_market_demand: The demand for railway conductors in 2026 is Strong, particularly in the Prairie provinces (AB, SK, MB) and Ontario. With the expansion of Canada's export capacity and the growth of e-commerce logistics, major carriers like CN and CPKC are engaged in aggressive national recruitment. According to 2026 Job Bank data, the industry is facing a "Generational Shift" as over 30% of the current workforce is expected to retire by 2030, creating thousands of openings for new conductors who can eventually promote to Locomotive Engineer (NOC 73310).
The "Promotion Fast-Track": Conductors are the primary talent pool for Locomotive Engineers, one of Canada’s highest-paid trade roles.
Modern Rail Safety Tech: Work with the latest Positive Train Control (PTC) systems and remote-control locomotive technology (Beltpack).
Premier Union Benefits: Most conductors belong to the Teamsters Canada Rail Conference, ensuring exceptional health, dental, and defined-benefit pension plans.
Strategic PR Pathways: This NOC is a priority for 2026 Transport Category draws, allowing skilled workers to secure Permanent Residency with significantly lower CRS scores than general applicants.
pathways: Express Entry (Transport Category Selection): A high-priority NOC for 2026 category-based draws with lower CRS requirements.
Federal Skilled Trades Program (FSTP): A reliable route for conductors with 2 years of experience and a valid job offer or Canadian certification.
OINP Skilled Trades Stream: Frequently targets conductors already working within Ontario’s extensive rail network.
Alberta Opportunity Stream: Ideal for those currently employed by major rail carriers within Alberta with a valid job offer.
